prompt:
The batch report renderer is producing the right bytes, but its CPU profile regressed when report sizes grew. Inspect the included baseline profiler trace at `profiles/report_renderer.prof.txt`, identify the repeated work in the rendering hot path, and fix the focused performance defect in `report_renderer.py`.

`ReportTemplate` is the immutable owner of its source text. Cache parsed template data at that boundary so one template instance can be reused across records and across `ReportRenderer` instances without reparsing. Do not introduce a process-global cache: two separately constructed template objects must retain independent parse lifecycles even when their source strings are equal. Preserve exact rendered UTF-8 bytes, record order, placeholder behavior, missing-field errors, and the immutability of template source.

Performance acceptance is deterministic and call-count based, not timing based: rendering a nonempty report repeatedly with the same template may invoke `parse_template` at most once for that template instance, while each separately constructed and used template is parsed once. Do not modify `protected_tests/` or the profiler trace. Use only the Python standard library and do not add network access, randomness, clocks, sleeps, subprocesses, or external dependencies. Run `env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -B -m unittest discover -s protected_tests -p 'test_*.py' -v` from the repository root.
verify_cmd: env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -B -m unittest discover -s protected_tests -p 'test_*.py' -v

full_distill.primary_skill: read an included deterministic profiler trace, locate repeated template parsing in a report-rendering hot path, and cache compiled data on the immutable template instance
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the protected suite preserves exact report bytes but fails its deterministic parser-call budget because a 40-record report rendered twice invokes parse_template 80 times instead of at most once
full_distill.success_contract: an immutable ReportTemplate parses at most once across records and renderer instances, separately constructed templates have independent parse lifecycles, and all output and error behavior stays byte-for-byte identical
adversarial_factors: the protected profile names parse_template as a 5000-call descendant of render, directing the repair to measured repeated work rather than wall-clock guesswork
the same template is rendered by two ReportRenderer instances, rejecting a renderer-local cache whose lifetime is too narrow
two distinct templates with equal source must each parse exactly once, rejecting a process-global or source-keyed cache at the wrong ownership boundary
golden UTF-8 bytes, repeated placeholders, empty input, and exact KeyError behavior reject output-changing parser or renderer rewrites, while frozen-source enforcement keeps the cache key immutable
the call budget is instrumented with unittest.mock and contains no timing threshold, making acceptance deterministic across machines
